Article normalization can create false matches if suffixes, leading zeros, or service characters are stripped too aggressively.
Fuzzy name matching can confuse side, size, kit, pack, material, connector, or category differences; conservative thresholds and conflict flags are mandatory.
Brand dictionary aliases may be incomplete or ambiguous until real customer nomenclature is inspected.
Category extraction may remain weak for unknown or excluded categories; hidden-cross safety defaults must remain blocked.
Adding fuzzy-matching dependencies may require PyPI or a package mirror; if blocked, use standard-library algorithms first and record limitations.
PostgreSQL duplicate-candidate generation may need indexes or `pg_trgm` later; do not add database extensions without verification and migration evidence.
A minimal review service is not the same as a polished review UI; avoid claiming UX completeness unless implemented and verified.

Count self-checks can become noisy if validation/normalization limits are not explained clearly; warnings must include reasons and local diagnostic details.

1C analog text may contain inconsistent delimiters, old part numbers, brand fragments, or free-form comments, so relationship extraction must preserve raw text and route ambiguous cases to review/history rather than invent certainty.
Preferred-item grouping can hide context if duplicate source items are not still visible from the result/card.
Non-stock search offer groups can create false confidence if proposed alternatives or suspicious-similar items are visually mixed with confirmed duplicates or customer 1C analogs.
Adding a frontend stack too early may distract from backend/search/card contracts; UI handoff should happen only after contracts are precise enough.

Real DB-data validation may reveal mapping, search-ranking, label, or data-quality issues that require tuning before checkpoint acceptance.

License rules may forbid caching, display, export, or retention; implementation must block those actions rather than hiding the source limitation.
Supplier/distributor data may contain availability or marketing claims but not official fitment truth.
Source conflicts can create false confidence if only positive evidence is shown; negative and conflicting evidence must be preserved.
Official cross precision cannot be proven without a human-reviewed sample.
Reference files can accidentally contain private or licensed catalog data; keep real files ignored and use only safe fixtures in git.
